game looks so cool So with this in mind pearl abyss have been ramping up its reveals and showcasing more about this mysterious game That features insane combat dragons and even a gta style crime system Recently in an interview for the new game plus showcase with content creators like luke stevens and duality as well as pr developer will powers We got a deeper look at the game as well as some of those burning questions now answered like how big is the game What is the story actually about and why did they switch from an mmo? So in this video, we've summed up the best part so you can get a better idea about the game and let's dive in A big mystery of the game has been the size and scale of it and how it compares to other games in terms of content Well in the interview the developer actually answered a question about this They said not only is the map larger than those of skyrim and red dead Which are some of the biggest open world games on console? But also for those concerned about the world just being a massive tick list They have actually filled the world with a ton of interesting and interactive content So the world itself doesn't feel barren like some of the other big open world games out there Hopefully they can actually live up to that promise because it does look like the world has some amazing things to explore Especially with some other crazy things like the dragons and the crime system and so on Now we do know a lot of you are keen to learn more about the story itself and the Impacts that we will have on it as players and since the reveal of rooms and desert It's been clear that the main narrative revolves around cliff and the gray mains making their way through a brutal and war-torn Continent that's on the brink of collapse after the death of the king in the interview They elaborated on this a bit by explaining that at the start of the game Cliff and the gray mains are the protectors of a particular faction that comes under attack from a rival faction They basically get ruined by them and some of them end up scattered around the world And the game is about getting them back and regrouping as well as reclaiming your power with it being an rpg One of the interviewers were interested to know whether or not you'll be able to romance characters Well, the developer did clarify that this isn't that type of game Which we're guessing it isn't like mass effect where you're able to romance NPCs or companions And there's a ton of different branching dialogue options He said that players would experience the same story But the choices that they make throughout the emergent gameplay and the way that they build cliff the main character Will make the game feel massively different for each player Now something that leads on from that and that was mentioned in different interviews Which is super interesting to us is that if you've looked at all of the gameplay in the showcases and the demos out there Well, you may have an idea in your head that this is everything that the game is about But the pearl abyss rep spoke about the demos so far that they've released and they don't showcase the at-home Experience very well because in these demos the characters are boosted and they've been more specced into an all-rounder Rather than a specific or particular play style Obviously, it's been done to showcase the different things that you can do in the game But in the actual final game that we will get to play If you want to spec into a certain play style like say becoming a god with the bow and Specking more into mobility and that while forgoing melee It seems like that will be something you're able to do So you can really nail down into your particular play style Another question asked was what you can do during the quiet times in the game And it was a really interesting one as the developer spoke about how the game will be including life skills Which we've seen some of them already like fishing cooking and mining But he said they haven't done the greatest job of showcasing those systems yet However, he did say there is still a bit of time before launch to showcase these and to give everyone a better idea They then spoke about the crafting system Which is something we're really keen to know more about because it seems like it will lead to crafting better weapons Types and maybe even being able to get a house in the game for crafting They said so far they've only shown the manual system for collecting resources such as individually chopping down trees Or manually smashing up rocks then the full game It sounds like players will be able to unlock more methods and automatic methods of collecting resources With the most recent comparison point that we can think of being death stranding Where you have various ways to it.
